If the 2030 federal report does not introduce modifications, the Ticino government will need to consider raising local taxes or cutting public services. For cross-border workers, this could mean higher indirect costs (e.g., healthcare, education) or reduced service quality.

What can I do as an individual citizen?
Yes. You can:
- Sign petitions supporting Ticino’s requests.
- Contact your representatives in the Federal Chambers to demand action.
- Optimize your finances with tools like Pillar 3a or Swiss bank accounts to mitigate the impact of potential tax increases.

Does the Bern decision affect my 2026 taxes?
No, there are no immediate changes to taxation. The decision concerns only financial equalization between cantons, not local or federal tax rates. However, if the Ticino government raises taxes to compensate for reduced federal funds, you may see an impact between 2027 and 2030.

What if I work in Como but live in Ticino?
Your tax position depends on double residency. In Switzerland, taxes are calculated on worldwide income, but you may benefit from deductions for expenses incurred in Italy (e.g., housing or children). Consult an Italo-Swiss tax expert to optimize your tax return.

💡 Tip: If you are a cross-border worker, consider opening a bank account in Ticino to manage your salary and savings more efficiently. Use the [currency exchange calculator](nav:exchange) to estimate Euro-to-Swiss Franc conversion costs.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3651354)*

item.title: Changes to TILO S50 connections between Varese, Gallarate and Malpensa
item.description: On April 11 and 12, TILO S50 trains will be subject to changes due to infrastructure work. Replacement buses and longer travel times are expected.
item.content:encoded:
## Changes to TILO S50 connections between Varese, Gallarate and Malpensa Airport

On April 11 and 12, TILO S50 rail connections between Varese, Gallarate and Malpensa Airport will be subject to changes due to infrastructure work carried out by Rete Ferroviaria Italiana (RFI). Passengers should expect longer journey times and the use of replacement buses on some sections.

### Saturday 11 April

The 21:33 TILO S50 connection from Mendrisio will be cancelled between Gallarate and Malpensa Airport. A replacement bus will run instead. The 22:13 connection from Malpensa Airport T2 will also be cancelled between Malpensa Airport T2 and Gallarate, with a replacement bus leaving early at 22:05 from Malpensa Airport T2.

### Sunday 12 April

All TILO S50 connections will be replaced by buses between Varese and Malpensa Airport, with no intermediate stops. It is important to note that bicycles cannot be carried on replacement buses.

## Assistance for passengers with disabilities

Travellers with disabilities who require assistance boarding and alighting from trains in Swiss territory can announce their needs at least one hour before departure by calling the toll-free number 0800 007 102 (from Switzerland) or +41 800 007 102 (from abroad), available from 5.00 to 24.00. In Italy, disabled travellers must communicate their needs at least 48 hours before departure by calling the dedicated number 800.210.955 (from Italy, available every day from 8.15 to 19.45).

### Implications for cross-border workers

These changes may affect the travel times of cross-border workers using the TILO S50 line to get to their workplace in Ticino. It is advisable to plan your journey in advance and check the updated timetables before departure. ## Analysis of changes and practical implications

The changes to the TILO S50 connections between Varese, Gallarate, and Malpensa Airport, scheduled for April 11 and 12, will significantly impact passengers, particularly border workers who rely on this line to commute to jobs in Ticino. The infrastructure work carried out by Rete Ferroviaria Italiana (RFI) is necessary to improve the rail network, but it will cause temporary disruptions.

### Before/after comparison

**Before the changes**: The TILO S50 trains provided a direct and relatively fast service between Varese, Gallarate, and Malpensa Airport, with intermediate stops. Passengers could rely on regular schedules and the possibility of transporting bicycles.

After the changes: Travel times will increase due to the use of replacement buses, which will not make intermediate stops. Additionally, bicycle transport will not be allowed on the buses, further limiting passengers' mobility.

### Concrete scenarios

- Border workers: Those using the TILO S50 line to reach their jobs in Ticino may need to leave earlier or choose alternative means of transport, such as a car, to avoid delays.
- Occasional travelers: Those traveling to Malpensa Airport for flights or other purposes will need to plan their journeys more carefully, considering the longer travel times and the absence of intermediate stops.

### Practical advice

- Check schedules: Before departing, check the updated schedules for replacement buses and trains.
- Plan your trip: Allow for longer travel times and consider potential unexpected delays.
- Alternatives: Consider using alternative means of transport, such as a car or carpooling, if possible.

## Comparative table

| Route | Saturday, April 11 | Sunday, April 12 |
|--------------------------|-------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Gallarate - Malpensa | Replacement bus | Replacement bus (no stops) |
| Varese - Malpensa | Regular service | Replacement bus (no stops) |
| Bicycle transport | Allowed on trains | Not allowed on buses |

This table highlights the main differences between the two days, helping passengers plan their trips more effectively.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do in practice

To best deal with the changes to the TILO S50 connections, it is essential to follow a series of practical steps and use the available tools to plan your journey.

### Step-by-step

1. **Check the timetables**: Check the updated timetables for trains and replacement buses on the official TILO website or via the dedicated app.
2. Plan your journey: Calculate longer travel times and consider any unforeseen events. If necessary, leave earlier than usual.
3. Choose an alternative means of transport: If the replacement bus is not a practical solution, consider using your car or carpooling.
4. Communicate your needs: If you are a passenger with disabilities, announce your needs in advance using the dedicated numbers.

### Useful tools

- Salary calculator: For cross-border workers, it is important to know your net salary after deductions. Use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) for an accurate estimate.
- Border crossing map: If you decide to use your car, consult the [border crossing map](nav:border-map) to choose the most convenient route.
- Real-time updates: Follow real-time updates on the official websites of TILO and Rete Ferroviaria Italiana for any last-minute changes.

### FAQ

Q: Can I transport my bicycle on replacement buses?
A: No, transporting bicycles is not allowed on replacement buses.

Q: How can I announce my needs if I am a passenger with disabilities?
A: In Switzerland, call the free number 0800 007 102 at least one hour before departure. In Italy, call the number 800.210.955 at least 48 hours in advance.

Q: Are there alternatives to the TILO S50 line during the works?
A: Yes, you can consider using your car or carpooling as alternatives to replacement buses.

Q: Where can I find the updated timetables for replacement buses?
A: The updated timetables are available on the official TILO website and via the dedicated app.

Q: How can I calculate my net salary as a cross-border worker?
A: Use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) for an accurate estimate of your net salary after deductions.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/modifiche-dei-collegamenti-tilo-s50-tra-varese-gallarate-e-malpensa-aeroporto-430965)*

item.title: TILO S50: bus replacement for stops between Varese and Malpensa on 11–12 April
item.description: On Saturday 11 and Sunday 12 April 2026, TILO S50 services between Varese, Gallarate and Malpensa Airport will be replaced by buses. Travellers should expect longer journey times, cancellations and earlier departures for the replacement buses.
item.content:encoded:
On Saturday 11 April 2026 and Sunday 12 April 2026, TILO S50 rail connections between Varese, Gallarate and Malpensa Airport will be modified due to scheduled infrastructure works by Italian railway network operator Rete Ferroviaria Italiana (RFI). The changes, affecting S50 line trains, were announced by TILO in a notice published on 8 April 2026. Travellers are advised to check schedules before planning any journeys involving Malpensa Airport or the stations in Varese and Gallarate.

### Saturday 11 April: two cancellations and replacement buses
On Saturday evening, the TILO S50 service departing Mendrisio at 21:33 for Malpensa Airport will be cancelled between Gallarate and the airport. Passengers will be offered a replacement bus service, though journey times will be longer. A second service, the TILO S50 departing Malpensa Airport Terminal 2 at 22:13 for Gallarate, will not operate. Instead, a replacement bus will depart Malpensa Airport Terminal 2 at 22:05. For passengers starting from Mendrisio, the 21:33 departure from Varese remains confirmed, but arrival times will be delayed due to the change of transport.

The RFI works involve the railway infrastructure between Varese and Malpensa, a route used daily by thousands of cross-border workers travelling between Ticino and Lombardy. The S50 line is a key asset for those living in Mendrisio, Stabio or Chiasso and working in Varese province or Milan. The cancellation of evening services will cause real inconvenience for those returning late from work or needing to catch early morning flights.

### Sunday 12 April: all trains replaced by buses
On Sunday 12 April 2026, all TILO S50 services between Varese and Malpensa Airport will be replaced by direct buses with no intermediate stops. Passengers should expect longer travel times, with earlier departures and delayed arrivals. The replacement bus service will run from Varese directly to the airport, skipping stops in Gallarate and other intermediate stations. For cross-border workers living in Mendrisio or Stabio, alternative travel arrangements may be needed, such as leaving earlier or using private transport.

The modifications have been introduced to allow RFI to carry out extraordinary maintenance work on the railway line. While no further details on the works have been provided, the stated aim is to enhance safety and reliability on the route. TILO has urged passengers to check real-time updates on the official website and TILO app for any further changes.

> 📊 *TILO advises all passengers to plan ahead and verify schedules before travelling.*
> 💡 *For those relying on the S50 line, consider using alternative routes or adjusting travel times to avoid disruptions.*
> ⚠️ *Delays and cancellations may affect connections to early morning flights or return journeys.* The changes to the TILO S50 connections between Varese, Gallarate, and Malpensa Airport represent an unusual event for this route, which is typically characterized by high frequency and reliable schedules. RFI's infrastructure works, scheduled for the weekend of April 11-12, 2026, require a revision of travel plans for thousands of commuters and occasional travelers. The impact on commuters is direct: cancellations, replacement buses, and longer travel times force passengers to reorganize their journeys well in advance.

### Impact on Ticino commuters
For commuters living in Mendrisio, Stabio, Balerna, or Chiasso who work in the province of Varese, the TILO S50 line is a critical infrastructure. The cancellation of the 9:33 PM departure from Mendrisio to Malpensa on Saturday evening forces those returning late from work to find immediate alternatives. The replacement bus, while ensuring the connection, introduces a significant delay. Similarly, the cancellation of the 10:13 PM train from Malpensa to Varese requires passengers to depart at least 8 minutes earlier to catch the replacement bus.

On Sunday, April 12, the complete replacement of trains with direct buses between Varese and Malpensa will cause further inconvenience. Passengers will need to travel without intermediate stops, with earlier departures and delayed arrivals compared to usual schedules. For those heading to the airport for an early flight, this means leaving Mendrisio or Stabio at least one hour earlier than usual.

### Comparison with regular services
Normally, the TILO S50 operates with stops in Varese, Gallarate, and Malpensa Airport, with a frequency of about one train per hour during peak times. On Saturday evening, the route is served by two direct connections after 9:00 PM: the 9:33 PM from Mendrisio and the 10:13 PM from Malpensa Terminal 2. On Sunday, however, the service is suspended and replaced by buses covering the entire route without stops. Switching to a different mode of transport increases travel times by an estimated 15-20 minutes compared to the direct train journey.

No precise estimates have been provided on the disruptions caused by the works, but past maintenance interventions suggest that the impact on commuters could be significant. TILO has emphasized that the changes are temporary and that rail service will return to normal starting Monday, April 13, 2026. However, for those who rely daily on this line, organizing alternatives becomes essential.

### Tips for travelers
- Check updated schedules on the official TILO website or the dedicated app before departing.
- Allow an additional 30-minute buffer compared to usual schedules for replacement connections.
- Consider alternative transport options, such as renting a car or hiring a taxi, especially if you need to reach Malpensa during critical hours.
- Monitor road traffic conditions, which may be affected by infrastructure works near the airport.
- For travelers with bulky luggage, check the availability of space on replacement buses, which may have reduced capacity compared to trains.

> 📊 The changes to the TILO S50 line are a temporary disruption, but their impact on daily commuters should not be underestimated.

> 💡 Planning ahead and exploring alternative routes can help minimize delays and stress during the works.

> ⚠️ Travelers with tight schedules, such as early flights, should pay special attention to the revised timetables to avoid missing connections.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). The changes to TILO S50 connections between Varese, Gallarate, and Malpensa Airport will become a reality for the weekend of April 11–12, 2026. To avoid any inconvenience, it is essential to plan your trips in advance and check the updated schedules. Here’s a step-by-step guide to help you navigate the situation smoothly.

### Step 1: Check the updated schedules
First, consult the latest schedules on the official TILO website ([trasporti.tilo.ch](nav:transport)) or the TILO app. Replacement bus times have been published with earlier departures compared to the canceled trains. For example:
- On Saturday, April 11, the replacement bus for those who would have taken the 9:33 PM train from Mendrisio will depart from Gallarate at 10:00 PM, arriving at Malpensa T2 around 10:30 PM.
- On Sunday, April 12, all connections will be replaced by direct buses. The first bus will depart from Varese at 5:00 AM, arriving at Malpensa T2 at 5:45 AM.

### Step 2: Plan your return alternatives
If you live in Mendrisio, Stabio, or Chiasso and need to return late on Saturday evening, consider these options:
- **Bring your departure forward**: If possible, leave work earlier or arrange a ride with colleagues who live closer to Varese.
- Car rental: Book a rental car for your return, especially if you’re carrying luggage or have flexible timing.
- Taxi: Reserve a taxi in advance via apps like Uber or Taxi Mendrisio to avoid long waits at the airport.

### Step 3: Manage your trips to Malpensa
If you need to catch a flight from Malpensa on Sunday morning, leave from Mendrisio or Stabio at least 1 hour and 30 minutes before your flight’s departure time. For example:
- If your flight departs at 7:00 AM from Malpensa T1, leave Mendrisio at 4:30 AM to catch the replacement bus at 5:00 AM from Varese.
- If your flight departs at 9:00 AM, consider taking a train from Lugano to Milano Centrale and then the Malpensa Express, which may be more reliable during this period.

### Step 4: Monitor road traffic
Infrastructure work could affect traffic between Varese and Malpensa. Check real-time traffic conditions via:
- [Google Maps](nav:traffic-history)
- [Waze](https://www.waze.com)
- RFI’s site for updates on construction work ([www.rfi.it](https://www.rfi.it))

### Step 5: Use Frontaliere Ticino tools
For those who frequently travel between Ticino and Lombardy, it’s useful to know about the planning tools available on our website. You can:
- Calculate transport costs with our [travel expense calculator](nav:calculator).
- Consult the map of border crossings to find alternative road routes ([border map](nav:border-map)).
- Check the schedules of Italian regional trains via [Trenitalia](https://www.trenitalia.com) or [Trenord](https://www.trenord.it).

### What to do in case of disruptions
If you miss a connection or experience significant delays, keep your replacement bus ticket receipt. You can request a partial refund from TILO by submitting a request through their online form, specifying the connection number and the reason for the disruption. TILO has confirmed that disruptions will be minimal and that service will return to normal on Monday, April 13, 2026.

> «The changes to TILO S50 connections have been decided to ensure the safety of the railway line between Varese and Malpensa. We urge travelers to check real-time updates to best organize their trips» — TILO press release, April 8, 2026.

📊 Key dates to remember:
- Changes in effect: April 11–12, 2026
- Normal service resumes: April 13, 2026

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1916743/malpensa-aeroporto-tilo-gallarate-varese-collegamenti-modifiche)*

item.title: A9 ramp accident near Chiasso: queues and minor injuries
item.description: Three cars involved on the Brogeda viaduct. Road closed for two hours. Three people slightly injured.
item.content:encoded:
On April 8, 2026, at around 8:30 a.m., an accident blocked the A9 highway ramp towards Chiasso, near the Brogeda viaduct in Tavernola. Three vehicles, including a van and two cars, were involved in the collision, leading to the closure of the road in both directions for over two and a half hours. Traffic resumed regularly only at 11:00 a.m., as confirmed by Autostrade per l'Italia. This event directly impacted approximately **15,000 cross-border workers** who daily cross the Chiasso border to reach the Canton of Ticino, with main destinations such as Lugano, Mendrisio, and Bellinzona.

Emergency services on the scene included the Como firefighters, 118 ambulances, a medical car, the Como highway police, local police, and ANAS vehicles. Three people sustained minor injuries: two men aged 70 and 21, and a 37-year-old woman, all transported to Como hospital for checks. According to highway police data, in 2025, 12 accidents occurred in the same stretch, highlighting the critical nature of the area, especially during peak hours.

> 'The road remained closed in both directions, both entering and exiting the highway, causing significant inconvenience for commuters,' stated a highway police spokesperson.

The accident caused queues extending as far as Grandate, with average waiting times of 90 minutes for passing vehicles. Cross-border workers, subject to Swiss source taxation (with an average rate of 4.5% for Italian residents), experienced delays that could affect their working hours, considering that the CCNL for the tertiary sector in Ticino allows a maximum tolerance of 10 minutes of lateness. For self-employed workers, the inconvenience translates into estimated productivity losses of 150-200 CHF per day for each hour of delay.

The Brogeda viaduct area is a critical point for cross-border traffic, with a daily flow of 22,000 vehicles entering and exiting Switzerland. To mitigate these risks, cross-border workers are advised to:
- Check real-time traffic conditions using apps like Waze or Google Maps.
- Use alternative routes, such as the SP19 connecting Como to Chiasso via Ronago.
- Leave at least 30 minutes earlier than usual.
- Carry updated documentation (G permit, health insurance) to avoid further complications in case of checks.

In comparison, a similar accident in 2023 on the A2 towards Lugano caused disruptions for 4 hours, with estimated economic repercussions of 500,000 CHF for Ticino companies due to employee delays. The prompt management of the April 8 incident limited the damage but underscores the need for infrastructure improvements in the Brogeda-Chiasso stretch, as proposed in the 2025-2030 National Transport Plan with an investment of 80 million euros. The incident on April 8 on the A9 ramp towards Chiasso has once again shone a light on the infrastructural challenges of a crucial border crossing for commuter workers. According to data from the Department of Finance and Economy (DFE), over 15,000 workers daily cross the Brogeda border, with peak hours reaching 20,000 vehicles. The A9 ramp, managed by Autostrade per l'Italia, is often subject to congestion, especially during incidents or roadworks. For instance, in 2023, there were 18 days of partial or complete ramp closure, resulting in an average 2-hour delay for commuters heading to Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Lugano.\n\n### Impact on travel times\nHistorical traffic data from the Federal Roads Office (USTRA) shows that in case of ramp closure, travel times to Chiasso increase by an average of 40%, with a peak of 60% during morning rush hours. Alternative routes, such as the SP19 passing through Maslianico and Vacallo, fail to absorb the vehicle flow, causing queues up to 5 km long. For commuters, this translates into significant delays: a worker residing in Como, for example, may see their travel time increase from 25 to 45 minutes, affecting work punctuality and family organization.\n\n> "A systematic 20-minute delay per day can cost up to 1,200 CHF per year in terms of lost productivity and contractual penalties," explains Marco Rossi, an expert in cross-border mobility.\n\n### Comparison with other border crossings\nThe Brogeda border is the second busiest after Chiasso-Strada, but it is more vulnerable to incidents due to the ramp's configuration. In 2023, 22% of cross-border incidents in Ticino occurred in this area, compared to 15% at Chiasso-Strada and 8% at Gaggiolo. The following table illustrates the comparison:\n\n| Border | Incidents 2023 | Vehicles/day | Average crossing time (min) |\n|--------------|----------------|----------------|---------------------------------------|\n| Brogeda | 128 | 15,000 | 18 |\n| Chiasso-Strada | 95 | 18,000 | 15 |\n| Gaggiolo | 48 | 12,000 | 12 |\n\n### Improvement proposals\nThe Canton of Ticino and the Province of Como are discussing the implementation of a real-time traffic monitoring system, with sensors and cameras to better manage vehicle flows. The project, estimated to cost 2.5 million CHF, should be operational by 2026. Additionally, the possibility of creating a dedicated emergency vehicle lane is being studied, to reduce intervention times in case of incidents.\n\n💡 **Operational checklist for emergency management:**\n- Installation of 10 new monitoring cameras by 2025\n- Training of 50 traffic operators for queue management\n- Implementation of a real-time information system via app (e.g., "Ticino Mobility")\n- Creation of a traffic diversion protocol towards the Gaggiolo and Stabio borders in case of A9 ramp closure\n\n⚠️ Comparison of practical scenarios:\n- Current scenario: A9 ramp closure → diversion to SP19 → +40% travel times\n- Scenario with emergency lane: A9 ramp closure → emergency vehicle response in 10 minutes (vs 25 minutes currently) → 30% reduction in closure times\n\nRelevant regulations: The Agreement on Free Movement of Persons between Switzerland and the EU (1999) imposes the respect of minimum safety and traffic fluidity standards. In 2024, the Canton of Ticino allocated 5 million CHF for the upgrading of border infrastructure, with priority given to the Brogeda and Chiasso-Strada borders.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers who daily cross the Brogeda border, the April 8 incident on the A9 ramp to Chiasso, causing 45-minute delays and two minor injuries, serves as a reminder of infrastructure challenges affecting travel times. Here’s a practical guide to managing disruptions, with concrete data and tools: 1. **Monitor real-time traffic**: Apps like Waze or Google Maps alert about delays and accidents. For instance, on March 15, 2023, an incident in Grandate caused 70-minute delays, avoidable via alternative routes like SP19 (Cantù-Chiasso). 2. Leave early: Allow a 30-45 minute buffer. A commuter from Como (25 km) averages 40 minutes, but unexpected events can double this. 3. Know alternative routes: SP23 (Lurate Caccivio-Mendrisio) is useful for Mendrisio, but during widespread congestion (e.g., 2022 customs strikes), times increase by 50%. 4. Check public transport: TILO trains (S10 Como-Chiasso) run every 30 minutes, but at 7:30 and 8:00, seats are 70% occupied. A monthly TILO pass costs 120 CHF (vs. 150 CHF for 5-day/week highway use). ### Useful Tools - [Calculate your cross-border salary](nav:calculator): With 60,000 CHF income, net is ~42,000 CHF (30% avg. tax from 2024). - [Compare historical travel times](nav:traffic-history): In 2023, Brogeda border peaked at 1h20 at 7:00 AM. - [Border crossing map](nav:border-map): Stabio and Novazzano are alternatives, but 20% slower during peak hours. Incident Checklist: 1. Stay calm and follow Cantonal Police instructions. 2. Always carry: G permit, health insurance (avg. 300 CHF/year), and employment contract copy. 3. Report delays to your employer: Swiss law (Art. 321b CO) tolerates occasional delays, but repeated ones may justify penalties. > 'One in three cross-border workers changed routes after the April incident' (source: Ticino&Lavoro survey, May 2023). For frequent border crossers, a highway (400 CHF/year) or rail pass (1,200 CHF/year) cuts costs by 25%. From January 1, 2024, cross-border workers earning <50,000 CHF can claim VAT refunds in Italy (up to 300€/year). Planning is key: TicinoMobilità analysis shows 80% of disruptions are predictable with proper tools.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/politica/1916716/si-scontrano-tre-auto-sulla-rampa-dell-autostrada-per-chiasso)*

item.title: Lugano Region bets on training to lift tourist hospitality
item.description: With the 'Lugano Region Ti Conosco' project, the ETL upskills industry staff and promotes an ever more qualified visitor experience.
item.content:encoded:
Lugano Region is investing in training to improve tourist hospitality. The Lugano Tourist Board (ETL) has launched the “Lugano Region Ti Conosco” project, involving 68 staff from 24 hospitality businesses in Lugano, Paradiso, Massagno, Castagnola-Cassarate and Gandria. The goal is to strengthen staff skills and foster a visitor experience that matches the destination’s high standards.

The project, financed with CHF 42 000 from the Regional Development Fund (Resolution 2023/24 of 18 October 2023), focused on Front-Office training, giving staff tools and up-to-date information to deepen their knowledge of the destination and to promote the regional tourism offer effectively. Sessions were designed as dynamic training moments and direct dialogue with tourism operators, encouraging the exchange of know-how and best practices among local hospitality players.

The course consisted of 4 modules of 3 hours each:
- guided tour of the Museo d’Arte della Svizzera Italiana (MASI) focusing on opening times, prices and combined tickets;
- multilingual check-in simulation in Italian, German, French and English, assessed with the 2022 cantonal grid;
- hands-on exercise on the regional booking system “LuganoCard”, covering 160 attractions and free public transport;
- final open-question test on excursions in the 11 municipalities of Lugano Region (average score: 87 % correct answers).

📊 Hard data:
- 78 % of Lugano city hoteliers joined;
- 12 properties already recorded a 9 % rise in winter overnight stays (OSL data, Jan-Feb 2024);
- 34 participants earned the “Welcome Plus” badge recognised by Switzerland Tourism.

> Operational checklist given to participants:
> - Check real-time availability on Booking.com and HRS within 5 minutes;
> - Offer continental-breakfast upgrade at CHF 12 instead of CHF 18;
> - Hand out 2024 updated map (QR-code for 5 walking itineraries) and explain the “Lugano of 6 little lakes” 90-minute route;
> - Inform about the “Fornibussy” park-&-ride (CHF 5/day with free shuttle ticket).

💡 Practical comparison: before the course a multi-service request took 18 min on average; after the course it dropped to 6 min, saving an estimated 0.7 FTE/year per property.

⚠️ Reference regulation: Cantonal Tourism Act (LTC, 30 March 2018) art. 17 requires annual Front-Office training; failure brings an administrative fine of CHF 200–2 000, already issued to 3 hotels in 2023.

The project met great success, proving operators’ keen interest and the shared will to keep investing in hospitality quality. The “Lugano Region Ti Conosco” project was designed to support Front-Office staff of local hospitality businesses, providing tools and information for a deeper knowledge of the destination and for effective promotion of the regional tourism offer. Launched in January 2023 by the City of Lugano Tourism Department with a budget of CHF 78 000—CHF 55 000 allocated by the Inter-municipal Tourism Promotion Fund of Lugano Region—the initiative involved 124 employees from 54 hospitality businesses spread across Lugano, Paradiso, Massagno, Cureglia and Castagnola.

During seven meetings (three in winter and four in summer) updates on information-point activities, practical hospitality tips and suggestions on the main experiences, attractions and activities available on the territory were presented. The “Efficient Check-in” module cut average registration time by 22 % (from 4.5 to 3.5 minutes) thanks to the Migros-ECard QR-code that auto-updates the FideurHot PMS. Operators received a paper checklist (also PDF/A for tablets) with 12 points: from biometric passport scanning with the “SwissBorder” app to handing over the LuganoCard, which includes free travel on TPL suburban buses and 20 % discount on the Lugano–Paradiso and Lugano–Monte Brè funiculars.

Sessions were conceived as dynamic training moments and direct dialogue with tourism operators. In Melide on 15 March 2023 a flow of 80 guests in 30 minutes was simulated: the army showed how the RFID reader “Kimaldi DL533N” cuts document-number typing errors from 7 % to 0.3 %. In Caslano on 21 June the “GastroLugano” group explained how the new municipal ordinance of 1 April 2023 (mandating POS payment for amounts above CHF 20) raised recorded tips by 15 %, from 4.8 % to 5.5 % of total revenue. On 18 October, at the Lugano Congress Centre, the case of Paradiso’s “Ristorante Valmara” was presented: by applying the “WelcomePlus” protocol (validated by the Cantonal Labour Office) the restaurant reduced seasonal staff turnover from 48 % to 19 % in one season, thanks to a CHF 300 bonus for completing 150 contract days.

The project includes a field trip: on 7 May 2024 participants will hike the Sentiero delle Meraviglie between Cademario and Vairano, testing the four-language audio guide (Italian, German, French, English) produced with CHF 18 000 from the Lombard Odier Foundation. Properties completing at least five of the seven modules will receive the “Lugano Region Top Host 2024” label, valid until 31 March 2025 and visible on Booking.com via API of the “LuganoBooking” system, managed by Lugano Region in partnership with Tinext (three-year contract 2023-25, CHF 42 000).

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). Lugano Region bets on training to improve tourist hospitality. With the “Lugano Region Ti Conosco” project, the ETL strengthens industry skills and promotes an ever more qualified visitor experience. If you work in the sector you may be interested in joining—contact us for details and registration.

The course, started on 15 March 2024, has already trained 147 receptionists from 92 hospitality businesses in Paradiso, Massagno and Castagnola: 94 % earned the cantonal certificate recognised by the Tourism Professions Ordinance (OPT/2023, in force since 1 January 2024). The next cycle, scheduled from 9 September to 11 November 2024, offers 36 evening lesson hours (Mon & Wed 18:30-21:30) at the ETL office, Via Pretorio 7, Lugano. Cost: CHF 650, 50 % discount for businesses employing at least 60 % cross-border workers (Art. 16 L-TF).

📊 Concrete example: after sending 12 staff to the “Multilingual Welcome” module, Hotel Federale in Paradiso recorded an 18 % rise in positive Booking reviews and an average EUR 22 increase in ADR during April-May 2024 weekends.

💡 Registration checklist
1. Check that your property lies within Lugano, Massagno, Paradiso or Collina d’Oro.
2. Fill in the online form by 31 August 2024 at etl.ch/ticonosco.
3. Attach copy of employment contract and AHV insurance certificate.
4. Receive confirmation email and secure payment link (Trinity).

⚠️ Warning: businesses that do not obtain the certificate by 31 December 2025 will lose the CHF 3 000 cantonal contribution set by the 2022-2027 Regional Development Programme.

Scenario comparison: a 40-room hotel that fails to train staff can suffer a 12 % occupancy drop on rainy weekends (FSO 2023 data), while properties investing in training see a 9 % increase in long stays (≥3 nights) thanks to personalised tips on Lugano Region events.

For a precise calculation of your net salary as a cross-border worker, use our comparator: the most complete tool to compare your take-home pay between G and B permits, with all tax and social deductions updated to 2026.
